revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:常陸国風土記]][[zh:常陸國風土記]]
{{Other uses|奈良時代の地誌|和菓子|Hitachi Fudoki}} '''Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki''' (常陸国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}, corresponding to most of present-day {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. It was compiled in the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, starting in {{ill|713|en|713|qid=Q23523|lt=713|1=713}} (Wadō 6) and completed in {{ill|721|en|721|qid=Q23541|lt=721|1=721}} (Yōrō 5). The text incorporates oral traditions written in an archaic form of {{ill|Classical Chinese|en|Classical Chinese|qid=Q37041|lt=Classical Chinese|1=Classical Chinese}} with songs rendered in {{ill|Man'yōgana|en|Man'yōgana|qid=Q862803|lt=Man'yōgana|1=Man'yōgana}}.
== Overview == The compilation of the ''Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki'' was ordered by {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|qid=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}} through an imperial edict:<ref>"On May 2, 713: Attach appropriate characters (two kanji) to the names of counties and villages in all regions and record detailed information on the resources produced, such as gold, copper, dyes, plants, birds, fish, and insects. Write about the fertility of the land, the origins of place names, and local traditions passed down by elders." (original Classical Chinese text)</ref> The text adheres to the reporting format stipulated by the [[Ritsuryō|ritsuryō system]] and begins with the preamble:
:"The administrators of Hitachi Province submit this document, based on the ancient accounts transmitted by elders."
It was compiled by interviewing elders and organizing their knowledge by district, offering insights into the life and perceptions of early 8th-century people. The content describes the geography and oral traditions of nine districts: Niihari, Tsukuba, Shida, Ibaraki, Namekata, Kashima, Naka, Kuji, and Taga.<ref>Hasegawa Shinzo, "From the Dawn of Culture to the Age of Warriors," in ''The History of Ibaraki Prefecture,'' edited by Hasegawa Shinzo et al., Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2003, pp. 27–28.</ref>
The identity of the compilers remains unknown. The surviving text contains phrases such as "below omitted" (以下略之), suggesting it may not be a complete copy of the original but rather an excerpted manuscript. Some scholars speculate that {{ill|Fujiwara no Umakai|en|Fujiwara no Umakai|qid=Q1370638|lt=Fujiwara no Umakai|1=Fujiwara no Umakai}}, a governor of Hitachi Province and noted poet from the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|qid=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō|1=Kaifūsō}}'', may have been involved in its compilation. Others suggest a connection to {{ill|Takahashi no Mushimaro|en|Takahashi no Mushimaro|qid=Q840963|lt=Takahashi no Mushimaro|1=Takahashi no Mushimaro}}, whose poems in the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|qid=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}'' reference Tsukuba Mountain and other aspects of Hitachi Province during this period.
Of the original ''Fudoki,'' only those of {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}, {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|qid=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|qid=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}, and {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} survive, though none in their entirety. The rest exist only as {{ill|Lost literary work|en|Lost literary work|qid=Q1585442|lt=fragments|1=Lost literary work}}.
== Etymology of "Hitachi Province" == The text provides two explanations for the origin of the name "Hitachi": 1. "The province was named for its 'direct roads' (直道, ''hitamichi''), reflecting unbroken connections between mountain ranges, rivers, and bays, enabling seamless travel and trade." 2. During Emperor Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, he ordered the provincial governor, {{ill|Narasumi no Mikoto|ja|那良珠命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narasumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to dig a new well. The clear, pure water impressed the emperor, who washed his sleeves in it, leading to the name "Hitachi" (meaning "soaking sleeves"). This anecdote is echoed in the local proverb: "When dark clouds gather over Mount Tsukuba, they speak of Hitachi as the 'land of soaking sleeves.'"
During the time of the ''Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki'' compilation, the region was described as "a vast land abundant in marine and mountain resources, where people lived in prosperity akin to the land of [[Tokoyo no kuni|Tokoyo]] (paradise)."
== Related Figures == * {{ill|Yato-no-kami|en|Yato-no-kami|qid=Q6145889|lt=Yato-no-kami|1=Yato-no-kami}} – A snake deity and/or yokai described in the ''Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki''. * {{ill|Daidarabotchi|en|Daidarabotchi|qid=Q1157529|lt=Daidarabotchi|1=Daidarabotchi}} – A giant associated with the creation of the {{ill|Ōgushi Shell Mound|en|Ōgushi Shell Mound|qid=Q11431872|lt=Ōkushi Shell Mound|1=Ōgushi Shell Mound}}. * {{ill|Hodoki Mountain|ja|晡時臥山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hodoki Mountain|qq=}} – A mountain and its associated legends recorded in the text.
== External Links == * [http://nire.main.jp/rouman/sinwa/hitatihudoki.htm Oral Translation of ''Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki'']
== See Also == * [[Fudoki]] ''' {{ill|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|en|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|qid=Q11498726|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}} ''' {{ill|Hizen no Kuni Fudoki|ja|肥前国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Bungo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|豊後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo no Kuni Fudoki|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ''' {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
